verses.text: The word's burden falls on Hadrach, Damascus, Hamath, Tyre, and Sidon. Nations judged. God's eye is on all the lands around His people.
The word's burden falls on Hadrach, Damascus, Hamath, Tyre, and Sidon. Nations judged. God's eye is on all the lands around His people.
Tyre heaped silver 'as the dust,' but the Lord will cast her out and burn her. Wealth no shield. Riches cannot save a city from God's judgment.
Tyre heaped silver 'as the dust,' but the Lord will cast her out and burn her. Wealth no shield. Riches cannot save a city from God's judgment.
Ashkelon, Gaza, and Ekron shall fear; the pride of the Philistines is cut off; 'I will encamp about mine house.' Foes humbled, people guarded. God brings down the proud and shields His own.
Ashkelon, Gaza, and Ekron shall fear; the pride of the Philistines is cut off; 'I will encamp about mine house.' Foes humbled, people guarded. God brings down the proud and shields His own.
Ashkelon, Gaza, and Ekron shall fear; the pride of the Philistines is cut off; 'I will encamp about mine house.' Foes humbled, people guarded. God brings down the proud and shields His own.
Ashkelon, Gaza, and Ekron shall fear; the pride of the Philistines is cut off; 'I will encamp about mine house.' Foes humbled, people guarded. God brings down the proud and shields His own.
'Rejoice greatly... behold, thy King cometh... just, and having salvation; lowly, and riding upon an ass.' Palm Sunday foretold. The humble King enters in meekness, bringing salvation.

He shall speak peace to the heathen; His dominion 'from sea even to sea'; by 'the blood of thy covenant' He frees the prisoners. Wide and gracious reign. Christ's kingdom is one of peace, won by His blood.

He shall speak peace to the heathen; His dominion 'from sea even to sea'; by 'the blood of thy covenant' He frees the prisoners. Wide and gracious reign. Christ's kingdom is one of peace, won by His blood.
'Turn... to the strong hold, ye prisoners of hope'; He will render double. Hopeful captives. God promises double blessing to those who turn to Him.
'Turn... to the strong hold, ye prisoners of hope'; He will render double. Hopeful captives. God promises double blessing to those who turn to Him.
The LORD shall be seen over them and defend them; they shall be 'as the stones of a crown'; how great His goodness and beauty! Crown jewels. God's people are His treasured ornament.
